en.extract:
The bar-breasted honeyeater (Ramsayornis fasciatus), also known as the White-breasted honeyeater, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to northern Australia, with a breeding season from late spring to winter. It feeds primarily on nectar and invertebrates.

The '''bar-breasted honeyeater''' ('''''Ramsayornis fasciatus'''''), also known as the White-breasted honeyeater, is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].<ref>{{Cite book|author-link=Gregory Mathews|last=Mathews|first=Gregory|date=1923–1924|title=The Birds of Australia|volume=11|publisher=Witherby & Co|location=London|pages=370–374|doi=10.5962/bhl.title.126732|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/item/250938#page/448/mode/1up|access-date=26 November 2025}}</ref> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to northern [[Australia]], with a breeding season from late spring to winter.<ref name=":2">{{cite web|url=https://www.hbw.com/species/bar-breasted-honeyeater-ramsayornis-fasciatus|title=Bar-breasted Honeyeater (''Ramsayornis fasciatus'')|work=[[Handbook of the Birds of the World]]|publisher=Lynx Edicions|location=Barcelona|access-date=4 August 2019}}</ref> It feeds primarily on nectar and invertebrates.<ref name=":2"/>
